parsed.reason: Exact match on user/ticket/role_group + attestation=accepted + expiry_window per SRC-001/SRC-002/SRC-003 for REQ-001.
REQ-001 matches SRC-001 exactly for user/ticket/role_group/expiry_window, and SRC-002 confirms manager attestation accepted as required by SRC-003.
Request matches approval (SRC-001), manager attestation accepted (SRC-002), and satisfies policy requirements (SRC-003).
role_group mismatch (SRC-001 payment_template_read vs requested write; SRC-003 requires exact match)

SRC-001 shows approval only for payment_template_read, not the requested payment_template_write; SRC-003 requires exact role match plus expiry_window and accepted manager attestation, so current records are insufficient.

Mismatch between approved role_group (SRC-001: payment_template_read) and the requested role (REQ-001: payment_template_write), violating the policy requirement for exact role matching (SRC-003). Manager attestation and other fields are satisfied, but the role discrepancy requires escalation.
All SRC-003 requirements satisfied by exact matches in SRC-001 (approval+CHG-4102) and SRC-002 (rollback_owner=assigned).
REQ-001 matches the required policy fields in SRC-003, with all required approvals/dependencies present in SRC-001 and SRC-002 for CHG-4102.
All required fields (service_account=SA-4102, service=SVC-4102, role_group=deploy_prod, change_ticket=CHG-4102, rollback_owner=assigned) are present per REQ-001, SRC-001, SRC-002, and SRC-003.
SRC-001 approves only deploy_stage (not deploy_prod); SRC-003 requires exact role_group=deploy_prod match which is absent
SRC-001 only approves deploy_stage, not deploy_prod, and SRC-003 requires exact deploy_prod authorization plus rollback_owner=assigned and change_ticket evidence before proceeding.

Approval SRC-001 authorizes role_group=deploy_stage, whereas policy SRC-003 requires role_group=deploy_prod for the grant; the mismatch means the request REQ-001 lacks the required role-group approval and must be escalated before execution.
REQ-001 matches SRC-001 (scope/sub seats) + SRC-002 (billing valid) + SRC-003 policy.
REQ-001 matches SRC-001 exactly, billing is valid per SRC-002, and SRC-003 lists all required conditions as satisfied.

All required conditions are met: REQ-001 specifies the seat increase, SRC-001 approves scope seat_expansion on S-4103 for 25 seats, SRC-002 confirms billing status is valid, and SRC-003 lists the policy checks (subscription, scope, seats, billing) which are satisfied.
SRC-001 scope=renewal mismatches SRC-003 seat_expansion requirement; no matching approval record for action_type=seat_expansion

REQ-001 matches the requested seat expansion, and SRC-003 states the required conditions are subscription + scope_code=seat_expansion + seats_added + billing_status=valid; SRC-002 confirms billing_status is valid for S-4103.

Request scope (seat_expansion) does not match the approved scope (renewal) recorded in SRC-001, while policy SRC-003 requires scope_code=seat_expansion; additional approval is needed before executing SI-4103.

REQ-001 matches the requested limit change, but SRC-003 requires scope_code=api_limit_increase and the only scope evidence provided is SRC-001 with scope_code=plan_upgrade; exact authority/scope matching is not fully satisfied.

REQ-001 lacks the required scope_code (policy SRC-003 mandates scope_code=api_limit_increase), while SRC-001 only approves scope_code=plan_upgrade, creating a mismatch; billing status is valid (SRC-002) but the scope deficiency requires escalation before execution.

Policy (SRC-003) requires payment-rail approval scope_code=destination_token_release, but SRC-002 shows scope_code=vendor_profile_review, indicating a scope mismatch; invoice approval is satisfied (SRC-001) but the rail approval does not meet the required condition.
